query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Bottom line: No, the US did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6.** 

At the July 1, 2026 mandatory joint review, the US instead **declined to renew/extend** the agreement under **Article 34.7** — a distinct, much less severe action. USTR Jamieson Greer stated: *"The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed."*

Key distinctions and facts:
- **Article 34.6 (withdrawal)** — full exit with 6 months' notice — was **NOT invoked**; no withdrawal notice was filed.
- **Article 34.7 (extension)** — the US declined the optional 16-year extension, which **triggers annual joint reviews** instead of the single six-year review.
- The **USMCA remains fully in force** — all tariff preferences, rules of origin, investment protections, and dispute settlement intact.
- Mexico and Canada both supported the 16-year extension.
- Absent withdrawal or extension, the agreement is scheduled to **expire July 1, 2036**; the extension can still be confirmed "at any time" by the three heads of government.
- Next: US–Mexico bilateral talks (Round 3) were scheduled for the week of July 20, 2026 in Mexico City; Canada had not yet begun substantive text-based negotiations.

The exit-related rhetoric (including a Trump official warning the President might leave the deal) is characterized by analysts as negotiating posture rather than definitive action, since no formal Article 34.6 withdrawal has occurred.

sources.title: USMCA 2026 Joint Review: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews
sources.snippet: No withdrawal notice was filed. Article 34.6 (Withdrawal) allows exit with six months' notice; Article 34.7 concerns only the optional 16-year renewal. The US decision involved only Article 34.7.
